articles.title: Trump says Netanyahu has ‘no choice’ but to accept Iran deal
articles.summary: Trump says Netanyahu has ‘no choice’ but to accept Iran deal Trump has told the Financial Times that Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu will have “no choice” but to accept a deal with Iran, even after Tehran fired missiles towards Israel. Trump said he calls “all the shots… he doesn’t call the shots”, referring to Netanyahu. He added that Iran’s attacks on Israel had not changed his desire to reach an agreement.

articles.title: UK urges Iran and Israel to de-escalate after renewed attacks
articles.summary: UK urges Iran and Israel to de-escalate after renewed attacks UK Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper has urged Iran and Israel to de-escalate after renewed attacks threatened to widen the regional war. “The resumption of conflict between Iran and Israel is in no one’s interest,” Cooper said in a post on X. “Both sides must show restraint and de-escalate immediately,” she added. Cooper said “negotiations must continue towards the lasting settlement that we all need, for peace and stability in the region, and for the full restoration of global trade”.

articles.title: Iran’s top diplomat briefs UK, Turkey and Pakistan after Israel strikes
articles.summary: Iran’s top diplomat briefs UK, Turkey and Pakistan after Israel strikes Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has held separate calls with his counterparts in Britain and Turkey, as well as Pakistani mediators, after Israel’s latest escalation in Lebanon. Iran’s Foreign Ministry said the talks focused on the “latest developments” in the region and Tehran’s response to Israel’s “repeated violations of the ceasefire in Lebanon”. The diplomatic push came after Iran fired ballistic missiles towards northern Israel in response to Israel’s strikes in Beirut.

articles.title: Syria shuts southern air space following Iranian missile attack on Israel
articles.summary: Syria shuts southern air space following Iranian missile attack on Israel Syria has temporarily closed air routes in the south of the country, the official news agency SANA reports, following Iranian missile fire towards Israel. The Syrian Civil Aviation Organisation announced the restrictions on Sunday night. It also suspended all flight operations at Damascus International Airport for 12 hours.

articles.title: IRGC says missile attack on Israel was a ‘warning’
articles.summary: IRGC says missile attack on Israel was a ‘warning’ Iran’s Revolutionary Guards say their missile attack on Israel on Sunday night was a “warning” after Israel struck Beirut earlier in the day. The IRGC threatened a wider response if Israel carries out further attacks, raising the risk of a broader regional confrontation. “Tonight's operation was a warning. If such aggressions are repeated, the responses will be broader and will cover all US-Zionist targets in the region,” the force said.

articles.title: Iran says frozen assets are not US war spoils
articles.summary: Iran says frozen assets are not US war spoils Iran has rejected reports that the United States could use frozen Iranian assets to compensate Gulf allies for war-related damage. Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi said regional governments were “not in a position to demand reparations”, after Reuters reported that Washington could make Iranian assets available to Gulf allies for rebuilding and repairs tied to future damage caused by Iran. In a post on X, Gharibabadi said Iran’s assets were “neither war spoils for Washington nor a payment fund for its allies”.
